title: Myco _canon.yaml
description: Machine-checkable shape of a Myco substrate's `_canon.yaml`. Governing doctrine: docs/architecture/L1_CONTRACT/canon_schema.md. This JSON-Schema is a second mechanical check alongside the Python kernel's `myco.core.canon.load_canon` validator — IDEs that understand JSON-Schema (VS Code, JetBrains, Neovim) can surface violations while the user edits canon, before `myco immune` runs.

schema_version.description: Top-level shape version. v0.5.x canons declare "1"; unknown values trigger an upgrader lookup (`myco.core.canon.schema_upgraders`).

contract_version.description: The kernel contract version this canon was authored against. Must match `myco.__version__`'s contract form (e.g. "v0.5.9" or "v0.5.9-alpha.1").

synced_contract_version.description: The contract version the substrate has last been assimilated against. Equals `contract_version` when no drift. Hunger reports drift when they differ; assimilate + CS1 resolve it.

identity.description: Substrate self-identification. Consumed by every verb via `ctx.substrate.canon`.

substrate_id.description: Globally-unique slug for this substrate. Matches `[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]{0,63}`.

tags.description: Free-form affiliation tags. NOT project boundaries — L0 principle 2 forbids scope-gating.

entry_point.description: Filename of the agent entry page at substrate root (default "MYCO.md"; symbiont substrates may use "CLAUDE.md").

system.description: System-level invariants. Every verb that writes paths checks these.

write_surface.description: L1 R6 enforcement — `allowed` is a list of fnmatch-style globs.

allowed.description: Paths the substrate permits writes to. Any write outside raises WriteSurfaceViolation (exit 3) unless MYCO_ALLOW_UNSAFE_WRITE=1 is set.

hard_contract.description: Pointer to the Hard Contract rules + their count.

rules_ref.description: Relative path to the rules file (typically docs/architecture/L1_CONTRACT/protocol.md).

rule_count.description: Number of rules in the Hard Contract. Must match R1-R7.

no_llm_in_substrate.description: L0 principle 1 addendum — `true` (default) means the substrate kernel does not import any LLM provider SDK. MP1/MP2 dims enforce mechanically. Opting out (`false`) requires a contract-bumping molt.

package_version_ref.description: Relative path to the file declaring `__version__` (usually `src/myco/__init__.py`).

pyproject_dynamic.description: Whether `pyproject.toml` declares `version` as dynamic (reading from package_version_ref).

lint.description: Lint-surface declaration. `dimensions` mirrors the Python-side registry; `exit_policy.default` seeds the default `--exit-on` spec.

categories.description: The four fixed categories from L1 exit_codes.md. Must be this exact set.

dimensions.description: dimension_id → category. Declarative SSoT paired with the class attr `Dimension.category`.

skeleton_downgrade.description: Fresh-substrate severity downgrade. Keyed on the autoseeded marker file.

marker.description: Path (relative) whose presence marks the substrate as skeleton mode.

affected_dimensions.description: Dimension ids whose severity downgrades in skeleton mode. Empty at v0.5.x.

subsystems.description: L2 subsystem declarations. Each key is a canonical subsystem name; optional `doc` points at the L2 doctrine page, optional `package` points at the code tree. Both are additive — fresh substrates often declare subsystems without either (kernel's `load_canon` does not require them; only the subsystem NAMES are checked against MF1).

doc.description: Optional relative path to the L2 doctrine page.

package.description: Optional relative path to the code package.

manifest_ref.description: Relative path to the verb manifest YAML (`src/myco/surface/manifest.yaml`).

metrics.description: Substrate-cited numbers. SE2 cross-checks these against observed reality.

current.description: Monotonic wave counter. Always increases on molt.

log_ref.description: Relative path to the contract changelog.
